title: Build accident detection system with manual reporting and AI image analysis

requirements.text: Create a manual accident reporting interface that allows users to quickly report accidents they witness, capturing essential details like timestamp and description

quotes: users manually report accidents they see

acceptanceCriteria: Users can access a report accident button or form
Form captures accident description and timestamp
Form integrates with location marking from the interactive map
Submission stores the accident report to the backend

requirements.text: Implement image upload functionality that allows users to upload accident scene photos for AI-powered analysis to detect and confirm accidents

quotes: the system to analyze uploaded accident scene images

acceptanceCriteria: Users can upload one or multiple images of accident scenes
Image upload supports drag-and-drop and file selection
Uploaded images are processed for accident detection
Detection results are displayed to the user with confidence scores
Successfully analyzed accidents are stored to the backend with images

requirements.text: Add AI-powered accident scene analysis that processes uploaded images using multimodal vision to detect vehicles, damage, debris, and other accident indicators

quotes: accident detection like the system to analyze uploaded accident scene images

acceptanceCriteria: System analyzes uploaded images for accident-related features (damaged vehicles, debris, emergency response)
Analysis returns a confidence score indicating likelihood of accident
Detection identifies key accident indicators visible in the scene
Results are presented to the user before storing to backend

requirements.text: Create an interactive map component with pin-dropping functionality that allows users to mark the precise location where an accident occurred

quotes: Drop a pin on an interactive map

acceptanceCriteria: Map displays an interactive view where users can drop pins
Users can click or tap on the map to place an accident location marker
Placed pins capture coordinates or location data
Location data is included when submitting accident reports
Map works with simulated location data since real GPS is not available

requirements.text: Extend the Motoko backend to store accident records including detection method (manual or AI-analyzed), location coordinates, timestamp, images, description, and analysis results
acceptanceCriteria: Backend defines an AccidentEvent type with fields for location, timestamp, detection method, images, description, and AI analysis results
Backend provides methods to store new accident events
Backend provides methods to retrieve all stored accident events
Accident images are stored using the existing blob storage system
All accident data persists across canister upgrades

requirements.text: Create an accident history view that displays all reported accidents with their locations, images, detection method, and timestamps in a list or grid format
acceptanceCriteria: Users can view a list of all reported accidents
Each accident entry shows location, timestamp, detection method (manual/AI), and thumbnail images
Users can click on an accident to view full details
List updates when new accidents are reported

requirements.text: Design a cohesive visual theme with emergency-focused color palette (red, amber, white, dark grays), clear typography for high-visibility alerts, and consistent layout patterns across accident reporting, map, and history views
acceptanceCriteria: Application uses a safety-focused color scheme emphasizing urgency and clarity
Typography is highly legible for quick scanning of accident information
Layout patterns create clear visual hierarchy between reporting, viewing, and mapping interfaces
Design conveys seriousness and reliability appropriate for accident detection

constraints: Do not modify immutable frontend paths: frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts, frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, frontend/src/main.tsx, frontend/src/components/ui
Use single-actor architecture in Motoko backend with all logic in backend/main.mo
Real GPS/geolocation is not available; use simulated location data or manual coordinate input
Do not integrate external AI services; use client-side multimodal vision analysis
Do not create driver alert system or 100-meter proximity warnings (explicitly removed from scope)
nonGoals: Real-time driver alerts based on proximity to accidents
Live GPS tracking or navigation features
Integration with external mapping services or APIs
Real-time accident notifications or push alerts
Multi-user collaboration or shared accident reporting
